description:
Your role: You are a database administrator who has been asked to simplify the way other team-members can switch between different data snapshots and configuration files.  To do that, you need to replace some hard-coded absolute paths with symbolic links, and then document those links in a small “audit” log.

    Starting situation
        • The following directories and files already exist:
              /home/user/db_data/bigdata/             (directory)
              /home/user/db_data/fastdata/            (directory)
              /home/user/db_archives/2023_09_prod/    (directory)
              /home/user/db_archives/2023_10_maint/   (directory)
              /home/user/configs/new_config.sql       (regular file)
        • No symbolic links with the names mentioned below have been created yet.

    Goal
        1. Create three symbolic links with the exact paths and targets listed here:
              a) /home/user/current_db_data          →  /home/user/db_data/fastdata
              b) /home/user/db_archives/latest       →  /home/user/db_archives/2023_10_maint
              c) /home/user/query_conf_active.sql    →  /home/user/configs/new_config.sql
        2. Produce a plain-text log file named /home/user/symlink_audit.log that documents the links.
           The file must contain EXACTLY three lines — one per link — in the following format:

              <link_path> -> <absolute_target_path>

           Example of a single valid line (do NOT include the comment character “#”):
              /path/to/link -> /path/to/target

           For this task the required lines, in this exact order, are:

              /home/user/db_archives/latest -> /home/user/db_archives/2023_10_maint
              /home/user/current_db_data -> /home/user/db_data/fastdata
              /home/user/query_conf_active.sql -> /home/user/configs/new_config.sql

           The file must end with a trailing newline after the third line.

    Constraints & verification notes
        • Do NOT move, copy, or delete the original directories or files; only create the specified symbolic links and the log file.
        • If a symbolic link with the required name already exists, replace it so that it points to the correct target shown above.
        • The automated grader will check:
              – That each symbolic link exists and points to the exact absolute target path.
              – That /home/user/symlink_audit.log exists, is a regular text file, and contains exactly the three lines shown above (byte-for-byte, including the trailing newline).
        • You should only need roughly three to four shell commands to accomplish everything.

    Remember: the task is complete when the symbolic links are present and the audit log matches the specification above.
truth:
Expected final state (used by automated tests):

    Symbolic links:
        /home/user/current_db_data      ->  /home/user/db_data/fastdata
        /home/user/db_archives/latest   ->  /home/user/db_archives/2023_10_maint
        /home/user/query_conf_active.sql -> /home/user/configs/new_config.sql

    Log file:
        Path        : /home/user/symlink_audit.log
        Permissions : 644 (regular file; any mode ≥644 is acceptable)
        Contents    :
            /home/user/db_archives/latest -> /home/user/db_archives/2023_10_maint\n
            /home/user/current_db_data -> /home/user/db_data/fastdata\n
            /home/user/query_conf_active.sql -> /home/user/configs/new_config.sql\n
